narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout

Technology.thesis: The Technology sector remains broadly bullish, primarily driven by the ongoing AI infrastructure buildout and increasing adoption of AI across various software and hardware segments. Despite some mixed signals and regulatory scrutiny, the underlying demand for AI-related technologies continues to fuel growth.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — forces a hawkish Fed repricing, killing duration bid for software
Major China export control expansion to advanced AI chips/equipment — significant supply chain disruption

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: The Consumer Cyclical sector is experiencing mixed signals. While luxury retail and certain e-commerce segments show resilience, rising interest rates and fuel costs are creating headwinds for broader consumer spending and residential construction.
watchFor: CPI for all items rises > 0.6% MoM again — further dampens consumer discretionary spending
Mortgage rates jump above 7% — severely impacts residential construction and big-ticket purchases
BLS Employment Situation shows unemployment rate rises above 4.5% — signals weakening consumer health

Communication Services.thesis: The Communication Services sector is mixed, with some areas benefiting from AI integration and content deals, while others face regulatory scrutiny and geopolitical headwinds.
watchFor: EU/US regulators announce new, stricter content moderation or AI usage rules
Major streaming service subscriber growth stalls or declines across the board
Digital advertising spending forecasts are significantly cut

Industrials.thesis: The Industrials sector is mixed, with Aerospace & Defense showing resilience due to government spending, while Heavy Construction Machinery faces headwinds from agricultural challenges despite some AI integration.
watchFor: US defense budget cuts announced or significant delays in major programs
Global economic slowdown significantly impacts freight volumes
Agricultural commodity prices fall sharply, further pressuring farm equipment demand

Healthcare.thesis: The Healthcare sector is mixed, with strong tailwinds from the GLP-1 obesity drug wave and stable performance in healthcare plans, but medical device companies face specific pressures.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors emerge with superior efficacy/safety profiles, eroding LLY/NVO dominance
Increased regulatory scrutiny or pricing pressure on GLP-1 drugs
Healthcare plans face unexpected increases in medical loss ratios or regulatory changes

Financials.thesis: The Financials sector is mixed. While some banks are leveraging AI and asset managers show confidence, rising interest rates and record private credit defaults present significant headwinds, especially for credit services and broader banking stability.
watchFor: CPI for all items rises > 0.6% MoM again — forces Fed to maintain higher rates, increasing default risk
Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% — signals increased credit risk and potential loan losses
Further surge in private credit defaults or contagion to public markets

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The Consumer Defensive sector is showing mixed signals. While discount stores are backed by Wall Street, broader consumer spending is facing headwinds from rising fuel costs and inflation, impacting even essential goods.
watchFor: CPI for all items rises > 0.6% MoM again — further erodes consumer purchasing power
BLS Employment Situation shows unemployment rate rises above 4.5% — signals weakening consumer health
Significant decline in gasoline prices — provides relief to consumer budgets

Utilities.thesis: The Utilities sector is showing bullish sentiment, particularly for independent power producers, driven by strong analyst reports and potential for steady growth amidst a data center boom.
watchFor: Unexpected regulatory changes impacting power generation or transmission
Significant increase in interest rates, raising cost of capital for projects
Slowdown in data center expansion, reducing demand growth

Basic Materials.thesis: The Basic Materials sector, specifically rare earths, is experiencing a rally driven by funding news and receding geopolitical fears, indicating a positive outlook for key players.
watchFor: China reasserts dominance in rare earth supply chain, impacting pricing power
Global demand for rare earths declines unexpectedly
New, cheaper extraction methods emerge, increasing supply

Precious Metals.thesis: Precious metals are currently neutral, with gold and silver prices reacting to geopolitical developments and inflation expectations, but lacking strong immediate catalysts for a decisive move.
watchFor: CPI for all items rises > 0.6% MoM again — reignites strong inflation hedge demand
Federal Reserve signals unexpected dovish shift — weakens dollar, boosts metals
Major geopolitical conflict escalates significantly — drives safe-haven demand
